rows.source_reason: Amsterdam Anion-Gap (bloed) numericitems rows are retained as the direct blood anion-gap source; non-anion calcium/procalcitonin false-positive rows from the broad Q4 scan are excluded.

mimic_source.main_source_selection: official chemistry.parquet as retained event source; raw labevents as audit-only source

rows.clinical_evidence: External check: anion gap is a calculated mEq/L or mmol/L measure; modern ion-selective-electrode ranges can be near 3-11 mEq/L, and ICU literature emphasizes albumin correction. Amsterdam p50 8.0 is therefore low versus MIMIC but not a scale failure.
rows.recommendation_reason: Direct Amsterdam blood anion-gap source is locked and scale is plausible; Amsterdam center is lower than MIMIC, consistent with formula/analyzer and albumin-sensitive anion-gap behavior, so keep a distribution caveat.

rows.source_reason: Amsterdam blood/plasma amylase rows are retained and E/L is treated as IU/L; urine, drain, ascites, pleural, and generic other-fluid amylase rows are excluded from this blood/plasma same-name candidate.

mimic_source.main_source_selection: official enzyme.parquet as retained event source; raw labevents as audit-only source

rows.clinical_evidence: External check: MedlinePlus gives blood amylase normal results around 40-140 U/L, while pancreatitis and other abdominal disease can produce large elevations. Amsterdam p50 73.0 and p99 1135.0 are clinically plausible for selected ICU testing.
rows.recommendation_reason: Blood/plasma amylase itemids and IU/L-equivalent source units are specific; other-fluid amylase families remain excluded and the distribution matches the approved MIMIC order of magnitude.

rows.clinical_evidence: External check: MedlinePlus gives lipase normal results around 0-160 U/L. Amsterdam p50 31.0 is normal-range centered and p99 2277.9 is compatible with severe pancreatic disease.
rows.recommendation_reason: Blood lipase source is specific, E/L is IU/L-equivalent, and the heavy pancreatitis tail is clinically expected without source-scale conflict.

rows.clinical_evidence: External check: Mayo Clinic reports typical ferritin ranges in micrograms/L, numerically equivalent to ng/mL, and lists inflammatory, liver, malignancy, transfusion, and iron-overload causes of high values. Amsterdam p50 344.0 and high tail are plausible in ICU selected testing.
rows.recommendation_reason: Blood ferritin source and ug/L-to-ng/mL equivalence are direct; the Amsterdam set is sparse and selected with an inflammatory high tail, so keep a sparse/high-tail caveat.

rows.source_reason: Amsterdam blood troponin T rows are retained; ug/L and ng/mL are numerically equivalent. The single other-fluid troponin row is excluded.

mimic_source.main_source_selection: official cardiac_marker.parquet as retained event source; raw labevents as audit-only source

rows.clinical_evidence: External check: Mayo Clinic Laboratories high-sensitivity troponin T reference values are sex-specific in ng/L, where 0.010-0.015 ng/mL is the same scale after unit conversion. Amsterdam p50 0.149 ng/mL is high but plausible because ICU troponin testing is indication-driven.
rows.recommendation_reason: Blood troponin T sources and ug/L-to-ng/mL equivalence are direct; Amsterdam values are heavily positive/ICU-selected, so keep a high-acuity distribution caveat.
